data.title: LIS Tech gets TN license for radioactive material at Oak Ridge; approved for uranium enrichment R&D with UF₆.
data.description: LIS Technologies has received approval to move forward with acquiring uranium hexafluoride (UF₆) feedstock and to progress in its research, development, and optimization efforts on its patented laser uranium enrichment technology of U.S. origin.

data.title: Datacentrex reports 48% quarter-over-quarter gross profit growth to $1.45M; revenue up 27%.
data.description: On December 23, 2025, in Carson City, Nevada, Datacentrex, Inc. (Nasdaq: DTCX), a tech-focused company, shared the unaudited performance results of its new wholly-owned subsidiary Dogehash Technologies, Inc. The third quarter of 2025 saw a notable 48% rise in gross profit to $1,450,986 from $977,066 compared to the previous quarter ending on September 30, 2025. This growth in profit was primarily due to increased digital asset mining capabilities and advantageous Dogecoin prices during that period.

data.title: AS Ekspress Grupp sells 100% shareholding in UAB Lrytas to UAB Admisa as per agreement on 23 December 2025.
data.description: On December 23, 2025, AS Ekspress Grupp finalized a sale and purchase deal with UAB Admisa. The agreement involves the sale of AS Ekspress Grupp's entire 100% ownership in UAB Lrytas (registration code 300781534) to the Lithuanian company, UAB Admisa.

data.title: Pledge Software launches Nonprofit Check Plus API for easier nonprofit verification. aim to reduce friction for platforms.
data.description: On December 23, 2025, in Dallas, Pledge Software revealed a new feature for its Nonprofit Check Plus API, known as automated developer onboarding. The aim of this update is to streamline the process for platforms needing to confirm the authenticity of nonprofits, especially in light of increased federal scrutiny. Pledge Software, the company behind Pactman (www.pactman.org), introduced this development to simplify the verification process.

data.title: Raytheon awarded $1.7B contract to deliver 4 Patriot units to Spain, Spain's largest order ever.
data.description: In an unprecedented deal, Raytheon, a subsidiary of RTX (NYSE: RTX), has secured its largest Patriot order in Spain's history. The contract, valued at $1.7 billion, involves the supply of four Patriot® air and missile defense systems to the Spanish military. The foreign military sales agreement signifies a significant milestone for Raytheon and demonstrates the growing demand for advanced defense systems worldwide.

data.title: NYSE Group announces holiday and early closing dates for 2026-2028 for its equity markets, including NYSE, NYSE American, and NYSE Arca.
data.description: NYSE Group, a division of Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (NYSE: ICE), a prominent global technology and data provider, unveiled the holiday calendar and early closing dates for its cash equity markets for the years 2026, 2027, and 2028. The markets covered include New York Stock Exchange, NYSE American Equities, NYSE Arca Equities, NYSE National, and NYSE Texas, along with NYSE American Options, NYSE Arca Options, and NYSE Bonds markets. This information is crucial for investors and traders planning their activities, with clear schedules for market closures and early closing days in the future.

data.title: Irondequoit, NY is the most competitive housing market in the U.S., with homes selling in 8.5 days on average.
data.description: In a recent report by real estate brokerage Redfin, it was revealed that Irondequoit, NY, a suburb of Rochester, has been identified as the most competitive housing market in the United States. Properties in this lakeside town tend to sell incredibly fast, with an average of just 8.5 days on the market, and often fetch prices well above their initial asking price. Following closely behind Irondequoit are Sunnyvale, CA, Santa Clara, CA, Tonawanda Town, NY (a Buffalo suburb), and Mountain View, CA. Interestingly, California dominated the list with four cities in the top 10, while three were from Western New York.

data.title: Pusan National University researchers find new photolithography method for high-res quantum dot displays.
data.description: A new nondestructive photolithography technique has been uncovered by researchers in Busan, South Korea, on December 23, 2025. This method provides a universal approach for quantum dot patterning, essential for enhancing the resolution of next-generation displays. Colloidal quantum dot (QD) light-emitting diodes show significant promise for applications in displays due to their high performance. This breakthrough offers a viable solution for achieving precise patterning needed for advanced displays.

data.title: DevvStream (Nasdaq: DEVS) to hold virtual Annual Meeting on Dec 29, 2025 at 10am PT, to discuss proposed business combination with Southern Energy Renewables.
data.description: DevvStream Corp. (Nasdaq: DEVS), a prominent carbon management and environmental-asset monetization company, is set to host its Annual Meeting of Shareholders on Monday, December 29, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. PT. The meeting will be held online. Besides regular agenda points, DevvStream's management intends to present shareholders with details regarding the recently revealed proposed business merger.

data.title: Massimo Group reports growth in dealer network, expansion, and strategy for 2026; provider of powersports and electric vehicles.
data.description: In Garland, Texas on December 23, 2025, Massimo Group (NASDAQ: MAMO), a company specializing in powersports vehicles and electric mobility solutions, shared details on its dealer network growth, organizational development, and strategic plans. The update highlighted the company's progress in these areas as it continues to enhance its operations.

data.title: Stock futures lower as market awaits delayed Q3 GDP report. S&P 500, Dow futures show minor gains. 10-year Treasury bond yield at 4.15%. Fed expected to keep interest rates unchanged. Stocks like Parsons (PSN) jump on new contracts. SPY down 0.019% to $684.70, QQQ down 0.058% to $618.85. Trading closing early on Weds, closed on Thurs.
data.description: US stock futures fell after Monday's higher close, with major benchmark indices showing lower futures. Economic data awaited includes delayed reports on third-quarter GDP and durable goods, industrial production updates, and December's consumer confidence. In the upcoming truncated week, trading closes on Thursday after a short session on Wednesday. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.15%, and the two-year bond at 3.50%. CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates an 80.1% probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged. Futures change for Dow Jones was 0.04%, S&P 500 was 0.07%, Nasdaq 100 was 0.11%, and Russell 2000 was 0.02%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust was down 0.019% at $684.70, and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF declined 0.058% to $618.85 in premarket trading on Tuesday. Parsons Corp. (NYSE: PSN) saw a 6.16% increase after securing a contract under the Missile Defense Agency's SHIELD program. PSN had weaker price trends in the short, medium, and long terms. Full story available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cipher Mining acquired 200 MW site in Ohio with 195 acres of land and PJM market participation approval.
data.description: The site acquisition involves obtaining 195 acres of land located in Ohio along with securing all required interconnection approvals to actively engage in the PJM market.

data.title: Norovirus cases surge during holiday travel with 122 million Americans expected to be on the move, causing 20 million infections annually.
data.description: During the holiday season, as 122 million Americans are expected to travel by January 1, cases of Norovirus are on the rise in St. Petersburg, Fla. This virus typically causes around 20 million infections annually. The increase in cases is particularly concerning due to crowded airports and festive gatherings, which can facilitate its spread.

data.title: Diginex (NASDAQ: DGNX) updates acquisition progress, emphasizing growth, with focus on ESG and compliance sectors.
data.description: On December 23, 2025, in London, Diginex Limited, listed on NASDAQ as DGNX, shared a strategic update about its acquisition roadmap. The Company emphasized its dedication to boosting growth, broadening market penetration, and enhancing its competitive stance in the swiftly changing ESG and compliance sectors.

data.title: Biomerica's inFoods® IBS Test receives $300 Medicare payment under CLFS, effective Jan 1, 2026.
data.description: Biomerica, Inc., based in Irvine, California, revealed on December 23, 2025, that the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) has set the national Medicare payment rate at $300 for its inFoods® IBS test under the Clinical Laboratory Fee Schedule (CLFS). This rate will be applicable to claims for the InFoods IBS test approved and paid for by Medicare with service dates starting from January 1, 2026. Biomerica, Inc. is a leading provider of cutting-edge diagnostic solutions globally and is listed on Nasdaq under the symbol BMRA.
